positive: The destination list includes at least one city or airport located in Germany
These destinations mention flying to Germany or a German city such as Berlin, Munich, or Frankfurt
The carrier serves a destination that is in Germany
negative: No destination in this list is in Germany; only non-German cities are listed
The text describes routes that do not include any German airport or city
Germany does not appear as a served country in these destinations

cascade.stage1_sql:
CREATE OR REPLACE TABLE mmqa.q6b_uncertain AS
    SELECT Airlines, Destinations
    FROM UNNEST(@airlines) AS Airlines WITH OFFSET pos
    JOIN UNNEST(@dests) AS Destinations WITH OFFSET pos2 ON pos = pos2
cascade.stage2_sql:
SELECT Airlines FROM mmqa.q6b_uncertain
    WHERE AI.IF("Given destinations '" || Destinations || "' of " || Airlines || ", the airline has flights to Germany.",
      connection_id => 'us.connection', endpoint => 'gemini-2.5-flash')

_ps_our_actual_bq.sql:
SELECT Airlines FROM mmqa.tampa_international_airport
    WHERE AI.IF("Given destinations '" || Destinations || "' of " || Airlines || ", the airline has flights to Germany.",
      connection_id => 'us.connection', endpoint => 'gemini-2.5-flash')

_repeat_runs._note: BQ AI.IF stochasticity dominates F1 variance. Royal Air Maroc (Casablanca) randomly judged Germany-positive ~50% of the time

_note: BQ AI.IF unstable on this query: Royal Air Maroc (Casablanca, Morocco) gets randomly judged as Germany-positive ~50% of the time. Both ours_BQ and ours_cascade F1 are 5-run means with std 0.16. cascade mean is marginally higher but well within noise — no statistically significant cascade win on quality
